Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

In the rapidly evolving world of Node.js, the choice of a backend framework can define the trajectory of a project for years. For a long time, the ecosystem was dominated by micro-frameworks — minimalist tools that give developers absolute freedom. However, as applications grew in complexity, a more “opinionated” contender rose to prominence: NestJS. While it has become a staple for many startups and enterprise-level companies, it has recently faced a wave of criticism from developers who argue that its rigid structure and architectural overhead might do more harm than good.
The debate essentially boils down to a fundamental question: Should we embrace the “standardized” way of building software, or does the flexibility of micro-frameworks offer a superior path to scalability? By examining the common grievances against NestJS and the counter-arguments from seasoned developers, we can uncover whether the “Jangan pake NestJS” (Don’t use NestJS) sentiment is a valid technical warning or simply a misunderstanding of architectural principles.
The Philosophy of Frameworks: Opinionated vs. Unopinionated
To understand why NestJS is polarizing, one must first understand the distinction between opinionated and unopinionated technologies. Micro-frameworks like Express or Fastify are unopinionated. They provide the bare…
